codeReview.summary: 16 well-structured tests covering all 9 validation assertions (VAL-PERFCHARTS-001 through VAL-PERFCHARTS-009) plus 7 additional edge cases. Mocks are clean — PerformanceTracker mocked as ES module default with all 4 methods, all 4 chart sub-components mocked with data-testid divs that serialize props for verification. Tests follow project conventions (userEvent.setup(), beforeEach with jest.clearAllMocks(), colocated __tests__ directory). The worker encountered and resolved a mock-return-value stickiness issue during development, demonstrating thorough debugging. All 16 tests pass, lint passes, full suite (327 tests) passes.

issues.description: The 'should switch active class to clicked time range button' test only verifies switching from 30 Days to 7 Days. It does not verify the 90 Days button or round-trip switching back. This is a minor coverage gap — the additional test 'should pass updated days prop to ActivityHeatmap when time range changes' does exercise all three buttons, but the active-class assertion is only done for the 7->30 transition.

The 'should call PerformanceTracker with new range when time range changes' test verifies getRecentPerformance is called with 30, 7, and 90, but does not verify that other PerformanceTracker methods (getWeeklyVelocity, getConfidenceDistribution, predictCompletionDate) are also re-called on time range change. The source code's loadChartData callback calls all four methods, but only getRecentPerformance is assertion-checked. This is acceptable since getRecentPerformance is the only method that receives the timeRange argument, but a withCallCount check would be more thorough.

The 'should hide prediction section when prediction is null' test manually re-calls jest.clearAllMocks() and re-sets all 4 mocks instead of using setupMocks({ prediction: null }). This inconsistency arose from a debugging session where the worker discovered mockReturnValue stickiness. While the test is correct, the approach differs from other tests that use setupMocks() and could confuse future maintainers.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker encountered a subtle Jest mock behavior: calling setupMocks() in beforeEach then calling it again in a test with different values didn't reliably override the prediction mock return value. The worker had to debug this with a manual clearAllMocks + re-setup. This is worth noting in the skill's mock conventions — jest.clearAllMocks() resets mockReturnValue, but calling mockReturnValue twice without clearing may not behave as expected in all Jest versions.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript skeleton shows 6+ tool calls debugging the null prediction test failure. The final fix was to add jest.clearAllMocks() before manually re-setting all mocks in that test (PerformanceCharts.test.jsx:240-253).

summary: Solid test implementation for PerformanceCharts component. All 9 validation assertions are covered by 16 tests with good mock hygiene. Three non-blocking observations around coverage completeness and a minor test-style inconsistency from debugging. No bugs or correctness issues found. Tests pass individually and as part of the full suite.
